violations.text: The menu does not have an allergen statement present for consumer awareness. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 25 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Reduced oxygen packaged salmon was observed thawing with the package intact. Salmon observed at 31 degrees Fahrenheit. Person in charge removed food from packaging. ROP seafood must be removed from the package upon thawing to prevent the growth of toxins. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Soup and baked goods packaged in the food establishment are lacking proper labels. Soups were observed without the quantity of the contents. Both soups and baked goods are lacking the major allergens. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine was observed with black and pink residue. The non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent the contamination of ice. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Several prepared food items in the Prep Unit next to the pass through were observed held at 42 to 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per the PIC, the Prep Unit was holding at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below prior to lunch service (Lower doors opened often and top lid kept open during service). Food items were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of the PIC, Steak, Chicken, Shrimp, etc. were placed hot in the stacked double door refrigerator. Pans were observed to be tightly spaced and not allowing for cold air flow. Food items were moved to the walk-in cooler for proper faster cooling at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2023

At the time of inspection, the front wall garage door were observed to be open. Outer Openings are to be tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. (PIC called company about screens to fit the garage door openings. Garage doors were closed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2023

At the time of inspection, dry food storage containers were observed to be stored under drainage pipe in the basement ceiling. Foods and food related items are to be stored in a way that protects from possible contamination from the facility. Food items to be moved to a protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2023
